findings.category: A04 Insecure Design / MASVS-PRIVACY (mic-open window during dismiss animation)
findings.title: Mic remains open during ModalBottomSheet hide animation on ON_PAUSE — ~200-400 ms recording window while app is backgrounded
findings.description:
`LifecycleEventEffect(Lifecycle.Event.ON_PAUSE)` (AudioRecorderBottomSheet.kt:245-250) calls `onPauseDismiss()` → `animateAndDismiss()` (line 121-129). `animateAndDismiss` launches a coroutine that `await`s `sheetState.hide()` (typically 200-400 ms ModalBottomSheet slide-down animation) before invoking `onDismiss`. The parent's `onDismiss` sets `showAudioSheet = false`, which finally tears down the composition and fires `DisposableEffect.onDispose` → `recorder.stop()` + `recorder.release()` (line 231-243).

Net effect: between the moment the OS fires ON_PAUSE (app backgrounded, incoming call, screen lock) and the moment the sheet actually leaves composition, the AudioRecord continues reading into the ByteArrayOutputStream for the duration of the slide-down animation. The recorded audio is then discarded on CancellationException (line 222-224, read-loop `throw ce`) and the stream is released with the handle (line 240-241), so nothing is persisted to disk, sent to VM, or transmitted — but the microphone hardware is physically capturing audio while the app is already in the background/paused state.

This violates the spirit of D14 ("drop the half-finished buffer on call/background/lock") by a narrow margin: the buffer IS dropped, but the hardware isn't silenced until the animation completes. On a device where the incoming call screen is already rendering on top, this is a ~300 ms leak window per dismissal. An Android 12+ device shows the privacy indicator throughout, so the user is visually notified, but AC-19 ("запись прекращается") is only eventually-consistent.
findings.impact: Narrow privacy window: ~200-400 ms of microphone capture continues after the app has been backgrounded by an incoming call or lock. The buffer is discarded; no data leaves the process. No network egress. Within the threat model of a local-first LLM app — flagging for Phase-3 hardening, not an exploitable leak.
findings.cwe: CWE-359 (Exposure of Private Personal Information to an Unauthorized Actor)

findings.title: `ErrorLog.e("audio", ...)` forwards AudioRecord / native-HAL exception messages that may carry SELinux / vendor-driver strings
findings.description:
`ChatViewModel.reportAudioError` (ChatViewModel.kt:292-295) passes the raw `Throwable` to `errorLog.e("audio", description, cause)`. ErrorLog serializes `cause::class.simpleName + ": " + cause.message.take(200)` (ErrorLog.kt:94-100). AudioRecord / native audio-HAL exceptions commonly surface messages like `IllegalStateException: startRecording() called on an uninitialized AudioRecord`, or OEM-specific strings mentioning `/vendor/etc/audio_policy_configuration.xml`, MIUI's `audioserver` denials, or HarmonyOS-specific audio-service rejections (R9).

These are not high-sensitivity, but they are device-fingerprintable and sit in on-device `filesDir/logs/errors.log` under 2 MB rotation — readable by `adb shell run-as app.sanctum.machina cat ...` and included in any future diagnostics export. Same exact risk profile as the Task-8 "camera" component finding (finding 3 of security-auditor-1 for task 8) — consuming the same closed contract, with the same 200-char cap in `ErrorLog.buildLine` as mitigation (D27).

"audio" is correctly whitelisted in `ALLOWED_COMPONENTS` at ErrorLog.kt:41, so `require(component in ALLOWED_COMPONENTS)` passes without throwing. No logging of PCM content anywhere — only exception metadata. Flagging for traceability only; no change required under D27.
findings.impact: Low — hardware/driver fingerprint in on-device error log. Requires adb / root access to read. No network egress. Within the threat model of a local-first LLM app.
findings.cwe: CWE-532 (Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File)

findings.title: `completed` AtomicBoolean guards `onSave` but not `onError` — a late read-loop exception can fire after a successful save or after Stop
findings.description:
The `completed.compareAndSet(false, true)` in `finish()` (AudioRecorderBottomSheet.kt:343) correctly guarantees exactly-once delivery of `onSave` across the Stop-button / auto-stop / dispose paths (the stated invariant in the KDoc at line 330-335). However, the error path inside the read-loop's try/catch (line 225-227) is NOT guarded by `completed`. The catch reads:
```kotlin
} catch (t: Throwable) {
    onError("audio record read failed", t)
}
```

Race scenario:
1. User taps Stop → `recorder.stop()` flips `recordingState` to `STOPPED`.
2. Read-loop's `while` sees the flip, exits, and calls `finish(...)` → `completed` CAS succeeds → `onSave` delivers the PCM to `ChatViewModel.addAudio` → sheet animates away.
3. BUT: if `stream.write` or the coerceAtMost math in step 2 throws after the CAS succeeded (partial I/O on the ByteArrayOutputStream is essentially impossible, but NOT impossible for a device OOM / JVM shutdown in-flight), the catch could fire `onError`.

More realistic: on an init-success + early-read-failure path (mic permanently denied by MDM mid-recording, USB-C mic hot-unplugged), the read loop's exception triggers `onError`, but because `finish` was never called, `completed` is still false — a subsequent Stop press (if the user is quick) races: Stop flips state, loop sees it post-exception-recovery? No, it already returned. Safe.

The only genuine risk is double-dismiss: `onError` calls `animateAndDismiss`, and `onSave` (via the wrapper in `AudioRecorderBottomSheet`) also calls `animateAndDismiss` — two concurrent `sheetState.hide()` calls. ModalBottomSheet's hide is idempotent, so no crash, but the `finally { onDismissLatest.value() }` block in `animateAndDismiss` fires twice, and `showAudioSheet = false` is set twice (Compose dedupes). Not exploitable, just messy. No PCM leak — both paths land in the VM: `onSave` stages the attachment, `onError` fires the snackbar. The VM's `addAudio` already TOCTOU-guards via `current.any { it is Attachment.Audio }` (ChatViewModel.kt:270), so even a concurrent second save is a no-op.
findings.impact: None exploitable. At worst: a redundant snackbar fires after a successful save on a narrow race. No PCM duplication, no memory leak, no mic stays open.
findings.cwe: CWE-362 (Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization)

findings.title: Permanent-denial heuristic re-runs the permission launcher on every sheet re-entry (same pattern as Task 8 CameraBottomSheet)
findings.description:
`AudioRecorderBottomSheet.kt:145-149` unconditionally calls `permissionLauncher.launch(Manifest.permission.RECORD_AUDIO)` whenever `hasPermission` is false at composition time. After a permanent denial the OS no-ops the launch and immediately delivers `granted=false` — snackbar fires, sheet dismisses. This is the intended flow and it is correct.

However, the `isAudioDenialPermanent` heuristic (line 372-373) returns `activity == null || !rationaleVisible`. On the user's very FIRST prompt attempt, `shouldShowRequestPermissionRationale` returns false by definition (the user has never been asked) — so the first denial is classified as "permanent" and shows the "Open settings" snackbar immediately, even though the user could have been re-prompted once more with a soft rationale. This is a documented trade-off (mirrored from `CameraBottomSheet.isCameraDenialPermanent` per the KDoc at line 366-371) and acceptable for Phase 2.

The same observation applies as the Task-8 finding 4: there is no throttling on how many times the user can re-enter the sheet. Tapping the mic button 5× after a permanent denial fires the launcher 5× and enqueues 5 snackbars. SnackbarHostState serializes them; today nothing logs on this path, so the amplification is purely cosmetic. No security impact.

Note that AC-20 (mic disabled when audio already attached) does NOT disable the button on permanent-denial state — a persisted `wasRequestedOnce` bit would be the right Phase-3 fix to distinguish "never asked" from "permanently denied", same scope as the Task-8 recommendation.
findings.impact: None today. Future-proofing: if `ErrorLog` ever adds a `permission-denied` component or a denied-too-many-times metric, the current flow will amplify it. Marginal battery / UI-thread cost from repeated sheet animations.
findings.recommendation: Same hardening advice as Task 8: persist a `wasRequestedOnce` bit and short-circuit the launcher at the ChatScreen call site for permanent denials. Not blocking for Phase 2 — mirrors the intentional design trade-off already accepted for CameraBottomSheet.
findings.cwe: CWE-1021 (Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers) — loose fit
